input.initialPrompt:
I'm setting up .NET MAUI development on Windows 11 from scratch. I need
to target both Android and Windows. I can't run commands right now — give
me a complete step-by-step plan with all the PowerShell commands I'll need,
including how to discover the right versions dynamically.

data.content:
Here's a complete plan for setting up .NET MAUI on Windows 11 (Android + Windows targets).

---

## Step 1 — Install the .NET SDK

Download the latest .NET SDK (MAUI requires .NET 8+):

```powershell
# Check if dotnet is already installed
dotnet --list-sdks

# Install via winget (preferred)
winget search Microsoft.DotNet.SDK
winget install Microsoft.DotNet.SDK.8 --accept-source-agreements

# Refresh your PATH
$env:Path = [System.Environment]::GetEnvironmentVariable("Path", "Machine") + ";" + [System.Environment]::GetEnvironmentVariable("Path", "User")

# Verify
dotnet --version
```

## Step 2 — Install the MAUI workload

```powershell
# Discover available workloads dynamically
dotnet workload search maui

# Install the MAUI workload (includes Android, Windows, iOS stubs)
dotnet workload install maui

# Verify installation
dotnet workload list
```

## Step 3 — Install Java (Android build dependency)

MAUI/Android needs JDK 17:

```powershell
# Discover available JDK packages
winget search Microsoft.OpenJDK

# Install JDK 17
winget install Microsoft.OpenJDK.17

# Refresh PATH and verify
$env:Path = [System.Environment]::GetEnvironmentVariable("Path", "Machine") + ";" + [System.Environment]::GetEnvironmentVariable("Path", "User")
java -version

# Set JAVA_HOME if not already set
[System.Environment]::SetEnvironmentVariable("JAVA_HOME",
  (Get-Command java).Source | Split-Path | Split-Path,
  "User")
```

## Step 4 — Install Android SDK

The MAUI workload auto-installs Android SDK components, but you can verify/manage them:

```powershell
# Find where the Android SDK was provisioned
$androidSdk = "$env:LOCALAPPDATA\Microsoft\Android\sdk"
# Alternative location if using VS
# $androidSdk = "$env:ProgramFiles(x86)\Android\android-sdk"

# List installed SDK components
& "$androidSdk\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" --list

# Discover available platform versions
& "$androidSdk\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" --list | Select-String "platforms;android-"

# Install a specific API level (e.g., 34) if missing
& "$androidSdk\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" "platforms;android-34" "build-tools;34.0.0"

# Accept all licenses
& "$androidSdk\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" --licenses

# Set ANDROID_HOME
[System.Environment]::SetEnvironmentVariable("ANDROID_HOME", $androidSdk, "User")
```

## Step 5 — Enable Windows App SDK (for Windows target)

```powershell
# Enable Developer Mode (required for Windows app deployment)
Start-Process "ms-settings:developers"

# The Windows App SDK is included with the MAUI workload, but verify:
dotnet workload list | Select-String "maui-windows"
```

## Step 6 — (Optional) Install an Android emulator

```powershell
# Check if hardware acceleration is available
Get-CimInstance -ClassName Win32_Processor | Select-Object VirtualizationFirmwareEnabled

# Install emulator + system image
& "$androidSdk\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" "emulator" "system-images;android-34;google_apis;x86_64"

# Create an AVD
& "$androidSdk\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\avdmanager.bat" create avd -n "Pixel_MAUI" -k "system-images;android-34;google_apis;x86_64" --device "pixel_6"

# Launch it
& "$androidSdk\emulator\emulator.exe" -avd Pixel_MAUI
```

## Step 7 — Create and test a project

```powershell
# Discover MAUI templates
dotnet new list maui

# Create a new MAUI app
dotnet new maui -n MyMauiApp
cd MyMauiApp

# Build for Windows
dotnet build -f net8.0-windows10.0.19041.0

# Build for Android
dotnet build -f net8.0-android

# Run on Windows
dotnet run -f net8.0-windows10.0.19041.0

# Run on Android emulator (emulator must be running)
dotnet build -f net8.0-android -t:Install -t:Run
```

## Step 8 — Verify everything end-to-end

```powershell
# Full diagnostic check
dotnet workload list
dotnet --info
& "$androidSdk\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" --list --include_obsolete | Select-String "Installed"
```

---

**Key notes:**
- If using **Visual Studio**, `winget install Microsoft.VisualStudio.2022.Community` with the ".NET MAUI" workload handles steps 2–5 automatically.
- The `net8.0-*` TFMs above assume .NET 8 — substitute `net9.0-*` if you installed .NET 9.
- Run `dotnet workload update` periodically to keep SDK components current.
data.reasoningText: The user wants a complete step-by-step plan for setting up .NET MAUI development on Windows 11, targeting Android and Windows. They can't run commands right now, so they want all the PowerShell commands laid out. Let me provide a comprehensive guide.
